newsSinceJun6: Stock jumped ~7% on June 8 to $324.45, an after-the-fact chase of Lam's June 2 BofA Global Technology Conference update (CFO Bettinger raised 2026 WFE forecast to ~$140B, SAM share to mid-30%, second Malaysia plant for growth through 2027). Today (June 9) shares faded -1.46% to $319.70 as headlines on new U.S. chip-equipment export curbs to China resurfaced; Lam derives ~35% of revenue from China, making it the most exposed of the major WFE names. No earnings, M&A, or company-specific contract news since June 6.

sources.label: Yahoo Finance - Lam Research raises WFE forecast at BofA Global Technology Conference
Yahoo Finance - How new U.S. China chip export curbs will impact Lam Research investors
GuruFocus - A look at Lam Research after 7.0% gain (price $324.45)

verdicts.rankRationale: On the node-criticality lens the WFE forecast raise to $140B and mid-30% SAM share confirm Lam's etch/deposition centrality to AI fab and HBM buildouts, but new China export-curb headlines hit exactly the export-control risk this thesis already flags; the two roughly offset, so hold after yesterday's hold.
verdicts.bull: WFE 2026 raised to ~$140B with growth seen through 2027 and a second Malaysia plant; Lam's etch/deposition tools are structurally levered to HBM stacking and gate-all-around node transitions.
verdicts.neutral: Equipment-spend centrality is intact but fully visible; the June 8 pop priced the WFE raise within a week, leaving the rank dependent on order timing rather than new discovery.
verdicts.bear: New U.S. curbs on chip-equipment shipments to China threaten ~35% of revenue, the largest China concentration among major WFE names, and memory capex is cyclical.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: HBM/NAND upgrade orders and next earnings confirming the $140B WFE trajectory
verdicts.primaryRisk: Escalating U.S. export restrictions cutting into the ~35% China revenue base

verdicts.rankRationale: On this crowding-sensitive alpha list, a +7% Monday chase of a week-old WFE datapoint, +72.75% YTD, +256% over 52 weeks and a ~$400B mega-cap leave little hidden alpha in an indirect beneficiary, while new China export curbs add a fundamental drag - residual asymmetry has shrunk.
verdicts.bull: Raised $140B WFE forecast and mid-30% SAM share give earnings upside through 2027 if AI memory and advanced-packaging capex stays hot.
verdicts.neutral: The indirect HBM/foundry capex thesis is intact, but it is now consensus and reflected in a $400B valuation; returns from here track the cycle, not discovery.
verdicts.bear: Most China-exposed major WFE name (~35% of revenue) just as new export curbs land, with momentum money piled in after a 256% 52-week run - asymmetry now skews against holders.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Upside surprise in HBM/foundry orders or relaxation of China export rules
verdicts.primaryRisk: Crowded positioning unwinding on export-curb escalation or a memory capex digestion phase

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's verdict already moved Lam down for the YTD rerating; the genuinely new items since then are offsetting - WFE forecast raised to $140B (positive) versus fresh China export-curb headlines on ~35% of revenue (negative) - so no second consecutive demotion is warranted.
verdicts.bull: Pick-and-shovel exposure just got bigger: $140B WFE forecast, mid-30% SAM share, and a second Malaysia plant signal multi-year etch/deposition demand.
verdicts.neutral: Execution and pricing remain solid, but at $400B market cap and +72.75% YTD the pick-and-shovel story is fully discovered; the rank already reflects that.
verdicts.bear: New U.S. export curbs to China hit the WFE name with the highest China revenue concentration, and a momentum-driven +7% pop on stale news is the kind of chase that reverses.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Next earnings print validating the raised WFE outlook and Malaysia capacity ramp
verdicts.primaryRisk: China export-control escalation compressing the ~35% China revenue stream
